panels.description:
Number of active CalculateRewardsV2 contracts, as seen by the sv app.

One such contract should be created when a round is closed, and should be archived as soon as SVs finish voting on the root hash of the reward merkle tree (usually within a minute).
panels.title: CalculateRewardsV2 contracts

panels.description:
Number of active ProcessRewardsV2 contracts, as seen by the sv app.

A few such contract should be created when the SVs finish voting on the root hash of the reward merkle tree, and they should be archived as the SVs create the corresponding minting allowances. Within a few minutes, there should be no ProcessRewardsV2 contracts left.
panels.title: ProcessRewardsV2 contracts

panels.description:
Stacked time series for the number of active RewardCouponV2 contracts, as seen by the sv app. Data is averages across all selected SVs, and disaggregated by contract age into 4 hardcoded buckets.

RewardCouponV2 contracts have a TTL of 36h by default, validators may decide to wait a few rounds in order to batch the minting of reward coupons.
panels.title: RewardCouponV2 contracts, average across all SVs, by contract age

panels.description:
Leaderboard of parties with hidden reward coupons.

Hidden coupons are created when a party is due rewards, but has not vetted the required package. The reward coupons are created without the party as observer in that case. As soon as the party vets the required package, the party is added as observer.

If you ever see any parties here, tell their validator node to upgrade or fix their package vetting.
panels.title: Parties with hidden coupons
Ingest verdicts and activity records (ScanVerdictIngestionService in scan app)

panels.description: The time difference between the last ingested verdict record time and the current wall clock time.
panels.title: Latency: last ingested verdict lag

panels.description: Rate of ingestion of mediator verdicts per second
panels.title: Traffic: verdicts ingestion rate

panels.description:
Average and 99th quantile batch size.

Verdicts are streamed in from the mediator, then batched in the scan app. For each batch we do one lookup for traffic summaries in the sequencer, and submit one SQL transaction to store all app activity data.
panels.title: Saturation: verdict batch size (10min running average)
Calculate reward merkle tree (RewardComputationTrigger in scan app)

panels.description: The time it took to complete a task
panels.title: Latency: median trigger task duration

targets.expr: sum by(errorCodeId, namespace, node_type, trigger_name) (rate(splice_trigger_attempted_total{namespace=~"$namespace", trigger_name=~"RewardComputationTrigger", statusCode!~"OK"}[$__rate_interval])) > 0

panels.description:
The time difference between the last ingested record time and the current wallclock time.

Note that the last ingested record time metric only updates when the store ingests a new transaction so if there is no activity, the last ingested record time will not advance.
panels.title: Latency: DbScanRewardsReferenceStore lag

panels.description: Number of batches in the latest computed round. Median value from all selected scan apps.
panels.title: Batches

panels.description: Number of activity records in the latest computed round. Median value from all selected scan apps.
panels.title: Activity records

panels.description: Number of parties with activity in the latest computed round. Median value from all selected scan apps
panels.title: Active parties

panels.description: Number of parties with rewards in the latest computed round. Median value from all selected scan apps.
panels.title: Rewarded parties
Voting on root hash (CalculateRewardsTrigger in sv app)

panels.description: The time it took to complete a task
panels.title: Latency: median trigger task duration

panels.description:
The time difference between the last ingested record time and the current wallclock time.

Note that the last ingested record time metric only updates when the store ingests a new transaction so if there is no activity, the last ingested record time will not advance. For a party performing reward collection, e.g., the validator operator party you expect at least one transaction every round so the lag should not go above 20min.
panels.title: Latency: DbSvDsoStore lag

panels.description: the time it took between the closing of a round, and this SV's confirmation for the CalculateRewardsV2 contract's processing
panels.title: Latency: time between round end and confirming root hash

targets.expr: sum by(statusCode, namespace, node_type, trigger_name) (rate(splice_trigger_attempted_total{namespace=~"$namespace", trigger_name=~"CalculateRewards.*Trigger", dryRun="$dryRun", statusCode!~"OK"}[$__rate_interval]))

panels.description:
Rate of BFT reads of the root hash. BFT reads happens if this SV node started ingesting verdicts after the round on which is being voted.

I.e., BFT reads should only happen after the SV node was onboarded or after its verdict store data was reset.
panels.title: Rate of reading root hash from other scans
Creating minting allowances (ProcessRewardsTrigger in sv app)

panels.description: The time it took to complete a task
panels.title: Latency: median trigger task duration

panels.description:
The time difference between the last ingested record time and the current wallclock time.

Note that the last ingested record time metric only updates when the store ingests a new transaction so if there is no activity, the last ingested record time will not advance. For a party performing reward collection, e.g., the validator operator party you expect at least one transaction every round so the lag should not go above 20min.
panels.title: Latency: DbSvDsoStore lag

panels.description: The time it took between the closing of a round, and this SV's processing of a ProcessRewardsV2 contract for that round
panels.title: Latency: time between round end and processing a reward batch

panels.description:
Note: this is a delegate based trigger. It is normal that this trigger fails due to contention as multiple SVs attempt to complete the same task at the same time. Example errors:

- FAILED_PRECONDITION: UNKNOWN_CONTRACT_SYNCHRONIZERS: The following contracts have been archived
- NOT_FOUND: LOCAL_VERDICT_INACTIVE_CONTRACTS: Rejected transaction is referring to inactive contracts
- ABORTED: LOCAL_VERDICT_LOCKED_CONTRACTS: Rejected transaction is referring to locked contracts

targets.expr: sum by(statusCode, namespace, node_type, trigger_name) (rate(splice_trigger_attempted_total{namespace=~"$namespace", trigger_name=~"ProcessRewards.*Trigger", dryRun=~"$dryRun", statusCode!~"OK"}[$__rate_interval])) > 0

panels.title: Errors: Failed trigger attempts

panels.description:
Rate of BFT reads of reward data. BFT reads happens if this SV node's RewardComputationTrigger did not (yet) calculate rewards for this round.

BFT reads here mean the SV is behind on calculating rewards, but still participates in creating minting allowance contracts.
panels.title: Rate of reading reward data from other scans
